claim: The 1954 CIA-backed coup against democratically elected Guatemalan president Jacobo Árbenz, carried out under heavy lobbying from the United Fruit Company, opened a 36-year civil war (1960–1996) that killed more than 200,000 people. The U.N.-backed Commission for Historical Clarification concluded in 1999 that about 83% of those killed were Mayan, and that 93% of human rights violations during the conflict were carried out by state forces.

sources.label: PBS NewsHour — “Timeline: Guatemala's Brutal Civil War.”
sources.quote: More than 200,000 people were killed over the course of the 36-year-long civil war that began in 1960 and ended with peace accords in 1996.

sources.label: Commission for Historical Clarification (Comisión para el Esclarecimiento Histórico), Guatemala: Memory of Silence (1999) — U.N.-backed truth commission report.
National Security Archive (GWU) — CIA and State Department records on PBSUCCESS, the 1954 Guatemala coup operation.

notes: PBS is a clean secondary that quotes the CEH numbers verbatim and links them to the 1954 coup. The CEH report (1999) is the primary truth-commission source. The NSA briefing book is the canonical paper trail on the coup operation itself.
